data.title: Morgan Stanley’s Ekblom Reaffirms Buy on Breedon, Lifts 12-Month Price Target to 474p on Improved Earnings Outlook and Margin Visibility
data.description:
Morgan Stanley analyst Cedar Ekblom maintained a Buy rating on Breedon today and set a price target of p474.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Cedar Ekblom has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Breedon’s Q1 performance and its earnings outlook. Revenue growth in the first quarter was broadly in line with expectations, with solid gains in the U.S. and Ireland helping to balance softer conditions in Great Britain, and Morgan Stanley’s FY26 EBITDA forecast now sits above visible alpha consensus.

In addition, management’s use of commodity hedging and contractual pricing mechanisms underpins margin visibility, even as residential markets stay difficult and growth shifts toward more robust non‑residential segments. Reflecting this improved earnings and risk profile, Ekblom raised the 12‑month price target from 448p to 474p, implying further upside from the current share price and supporting a constructive stance on the stock.

data.title: Landstar: Hold Rating Maintained as Analyst Lifts Price Target to $175 Amid Early-Cycle Strength and Legal Uncertainty
data.description:
Analyst Jason Seidl of TD Cowen reiterated a Hold rating on Landstar System, boosting the price target to $175.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jason Seidl has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including Landstar’s strong recent performance but balanced risk-reward. The company delivered better-than-expected Q1 results with unusually firm truck pricing for a seasonally soft quarter, and early Q2 data suggest that pricing and industrial demand are gaining traction into the spring.

He also raised his price target to $175, reflecting improved earnings power from rising revenue per load and early-cycle market strength, yet stopped short of a more bullish stance given remaining uncertainties. In particular, the upcoming Supreme Court decision on the Montgomery case could alter the liability and insurance landscape for freight brokers, and while Landstar’s compliance, vetting, and safety focus make it relatively resilient, the industry-wide policy risk justifies maintaining a neutral Hold view for now.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $155.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
